docs.title: Discourse CLI
docs.text: A command-line tool for Discourse forum administration via the REST API.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 17 command groups covering users, topics, posts, categories, groups, tags, badges, and more
  • 80+ subcommands for comprehensive forum management
  • Profile system to manage multiple Discourse instances (production, staging, etc.)
  • Flexible output with automatic format detection (table for TTY, JSON for pipes)
  • Generic API access via discourse api for any endpoint not covered by dedicated commands

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
uv pip install -e .

# Configure
discourse config init

# Start using
discourse users list
discourse topics list
discourse search "welcome"

docs.title: Example Workflow
docs.text:
# Set up profiles for different instances
discourse config init --profile production
discourse config init --profile staging

# Switch default profile
discourse config use production

# Or use a specific profile per command
discourse -p staging users list

# Pipe JSON output to jq
discourse users list -o json | jq '.[].username'

# Direct API access for any endpoint
discourse api GET /site.json

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Installation -- install via uv, pip, or from source
  • Configuration -- profiles, environment variables, and priority resolution
  • Output Formats -- auto, JSON, table, and piping with jq
  • Commands -- full reference for all command groups

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Discourse CLI supports layered configuration from multiple sources with a clear priority chain.

docs.title: Priority Order
docs.text:
Configuration is resolved in this order (highest priority first):

  1. CLI flags (--url, --api-key, --api-username)
  2. Environment variables (DISCOURSE_URL, DISCOURSE_API_KEY, etc.)
  3. Config file (~/.config/discourse-cli/config.yaml or platform equivalent)

docs.title: Interactive Setup
docs.text:
# Create a default configuration
discourse config init

# Create a named profile
discourse config init --profile staging

The init command prompts for URL, API key, and API username, then saves to the config file.

docs.title: Config File Formats
docs.text: The config file is stored at the platform-specific config directory (e.g. ~/.config/discourse-cli/config.yaml on Linux/macOS).

docs.title: Flat Format (simple)
docs.text:
For a single Discourse instance:

url: https://forum.example.com
api_key: your-api-key-here
api_username: system
timeout: 30
default_output: auto

docs.title: Profile Format (multiple instances)
docs.text:
For managing multiple Discourse instances:

default_profile: production
profiles:
  production:
    url: https://forum.example.com
    api_key: prod-api-key
    api_username: system
    timeout: 30
    default_output: auto
  staging:
    url: https://staging.example.com
    api_key: staging-api-key
    api_username: system

docs.title: Profile Resolution
docs.text:
When using profile-based config, the active profile is determined by:

  1. --profile / -p CLI flag
  2. DISCOURSE_PROFILE environment variable
  3. default_profile key in config file
  4. "default" as final fallback
# Use a specific profile
discourse -p staging users list

# Set the default profile
discourse config use production

# List all profiles
discourse config list

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Variable Config Key Description DISCOURSE_URL url Discourse instance URL DISCOURSE_API_KEY api_key API key DISCOURSE_API_USERNAME api_username API username (default: system) DISCOURSE_TIMEOUT timeout Request timeout in seconds (default: 30) DISCOURSE_DEFAULT_OUTPUT default_output Default output format (auto/json/table) DISCOURSE_PROFILE -- Profile name to use 
# Override with environment variables
export DISCOURSE_URL=https://forum.example.com
export DISCOURSE_API_KEY=your-key
discourse users list

docs.title: Config Options
docs.text: Key Required Default Description url Yes -- Discourse instance base URL api_key Yes -- API key for authentication api_username No system Username for API requests timeout No 30 HTTP request timeout (seconds) default_output No auto Output format: auto, json, or table

docs.title: Auto-Migration
docs.text:
When you create the first named profile on a flat config file, the existing flat configuration is automatically migrated into a default profile. No manual migration is needed.

# Start with flat config
discourse config init
# Writes: url, api_key, api_username (flat)

# Add a profile -- flat config auto-migrates to profile format
discourse config init --profile staging
# Now config.yaml has: profiles.default (old flat) + profiles.staging (new)

docs.title: Validate Configuration
docs.text:
Test the connection to verify your configuration works:

discourse config validate
discourse config validate --profile staging

docs.title: Show Current Config
docs.text:
Display the resolved configuration (API key is masked):

discourse config show
discourse config show --profile production

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or later
  • A Discourse instance with an API key

docs.title: Install with uv (recommended)
docs.text: uv pip install discourse-cli

docs.title: Install with pip
docs.text: pip install discourse-cli

docs.title: Install from Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/discourse-cli.git
cd discourse-cli
uv pip install -e .

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/discourse-cli.git
cd discourse-cli

# Create virtual environment and install with dev dependencies
uv venv --seed
uv sync --group dev

# Verify installation
uv run discourse --version

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
After installation, verify that the CLI is available:

discourse --version
discourse --help

docs.title: Output Formats
docs.text: Every data command supports the --output / -o option to control how results are displayed.

docs.title: auto (default)
docs.text:
Automatically selects the best format based on context:

  • TTY (interactive terminal): renders as a rich table
  • Pipe / redirect: outputs raw JSON
# Table output in terminal
discourse users list

# JSON output when piped
discourse users list | jq .

docs.title: json
docs.text:
Always outputs formatted JSON, regardless of terminal context. Useful for scripting and data processing.

discourse users list -o json

docs.title: table
docs.text:
Always renders a rich table, even when output is piped. Uses the Rich library for formatting.

discourse users list -o table

docs.title: Piping with jq
docs.text:
The auto format makes it easy to pipe output to jq without explicitly specifying -o json:

# Extract usernames
discourse users list | jq '.[].username'

# Count topics
discourse topics list | jq '.| length'

# Pretty-print a specific user
discourse users get alice | jq '.user'

# Filter active admin users
discourse users list | jq '[.[] | select(.admin == true and .active == true)]'

docs.title: Table Rendering
docs.text:
Table output adapts to the data shape:

  • List of dicts: columnar table with keys as headers
  • Single dict: two-column key-value table
  • Simple list: numbered rows

Nested or complex values are truncated to 80 characters for readability. Use -o json when you need the full data.

docs.title: Commands Overview
docs.text: Discourse CLI organizes commands into groups by resource type. Every data command supports the -o / --output option (see Output Formats).

docs.title: Global Options
docs.text:
These options are available on the root discourse command and apply to all subcommands:

Option Description --profile / -p Named config profile to use --url Discourse instance URL (overrides config) --api-key API key (overrides config) --api-username API username (overrides config) --version Show version and exit --help Show help and exit

docs.title: Command Groups
docs.text: Group Description config Manage CLI configuration and profiles users User management (list, get, create, update, delete) admin Admin operations (suspend, silence, activate, anonymize) topics Topic management (list, create, status, timer, invite) posts Post management (list, get, create, update, delete, lock) categories Category management (list, get, create, update, topics) groups Group management (list, get, create, members) tags Tag and tag group management badges Badge management (list, create, update, delete) search Full-text search across topics and posts messages Private messages (inbox, sent, create) notifications Notification management backups Backup management invites Invitation management site Site information and settings uploads File uploads api Generic API access to any endpoint

docs.title: admin
docs.text:
Admin operations for user management (suspend, silence, activate, etc.).

discourse admin <subcommand>

docs.title: activate
docs.text:
Activate a user.

discourse admin activate 42
Argument Description USER_ID User ID (integer)

docs.title: deactivate
docs.text:
Deactivate a user.

discourse admin deactivate 42
Argument Description USER_ID User ID (integer)

docs.title: suspend
docs.text:
Suspend a user. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes is passed.

discourse admin suspend 42 --until 2026-06-01 --reason "policy violation"
Argument Description USER_ID User ID (integer) Option Required Description --until Yes Suspension end date (YYYY-MM-DD) --reason Yes Reason for suspension --message No Message sent to the user --yes No Skip confirmation prompt

docs.title: silence
docs.text:
Silence a user. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes is passed.

discourse admin silence 42 --until 2026-04-01 --reason "spam"
Argument Description USER_ID User ID (integer) Option Required Description --until Yes Silence end date (YYYY-MM-DD) --reason Yes Reason for silencing --message No Message sent to the user --yes No Skip confirmation prompt

docs.title: anonymize
docs.text:
Anonymize a user. This cannot be undone. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes is passed.

discourse admin anonymize 42 --yes
Argument Description USER_ID User ID (integer) Option Description --yes Skip confirmation prompt

docs.title: log-out
docs.text:
Log a user out of all sessions.

discourse admin log-out 42
Argument Description USER_ID User ID (integer)

docs.title: refresh-gravatar
docs.text:
Refresh a user's Gravatar image.

discourse admin refresh-gravatar alice
Argument Description USERNAME Username

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Suspend a user for a month
discourse admin suspend 42 --until 2026-03-15 --reason "repeated violations" --message "Please review our community guidelines."

# Silence and then activate a user
discourse admin silence 42 --until 2026-02-28 --reason "cooling off" --yes
discourse admin activate 42

# Force log out a compromised account
discourse admin log-out 42

docs.title: api
docs.text:
Generic API access to any Discourse endpoint. Use this when a dedicated command doesn't exist or you need full control over the request.

discourse api <METHOD> <PATH> [options]

This is a top-level command, not a command group.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description METHOD HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH PATH API endpoint path (e.g. /site.json)

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description -p, --param Query parameter as key=value. Repeatable. -d, --data JSON request body (inline string or @filename) --list List all available API endpoints

docs.title: Simple GET request
docs.text: discourse api GET /site.json

docs.title: GET with query parameters
docs.text: discourse api GET /admin/users.json -p page=1 -p show_emails=true

docs.title: POST with inline JSON body
docs.text: discourse api POST /posts.json -d '{"raw": "Hello world!", "topic_id": 123}'

docs.title: POST with JSON body from file
docs.text: discourse api POST /posts.json -d @payload.json

docs.title: List all endpoints
docs.text:
discourse api --list

This reads the OpenAPI specification bundled with the project and lists all available endpoints with their HTTP methods, paths, and descriptions.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Get site settings
discourse api GET /site.json -o json | jq '.default_locale'

# Create a post via raw API
discourse api POST /posts.json -d '{"raw": "New post content", "topic_id": 42}'

# Fetch admin dashboard stats
discourse api GET /admin/dashboard.json

# Update a site setting
discourse api PUT /admin/site_settings/title.json -d '{"title": "My Forum"}'

# Delete with parameters
discourse api DELETE /admin/users/99.json -d '{"delete_posts": true}'

# Paginate through users
discourse api GET /admin/users/list/active.json -p page=0
discourse api GET /admin/users/list/active.json -p page=1

docs.title: backups
docs.text:
Manage backups.

discourse backups <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all backups.

discourse backups list

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create a new backup.

discourse backups create
discourse backups create --no-uploads
Option Default Description --with-uploads / --no-uploads --with-uploads Include uploads in the backup

docs.title: send-email
docs.text:
Send a download link email for a specific backup.

discourse backups send-email backup-2026-02-15.tar.gz
Argument Description FILENAME Backup filename

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List available backups
discourse backups list

# Create a backup without uploads (faster)
discourse backups create --no-uploads

# Send download link for a backup
discourse backups send-email backup-2026-02-15.tar.gz

docs.title: badges
docs.text:
Manage badges.

discourse badges <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all badges.

discourse badges list

docs.title: user
docs.text:
List badges awarded to a specific user.

discourse badges user alice
Argument Description USERNAME Username

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create a new badge.

discourse badges create --name "Contributor" --badge-type-id 3
Option Required Description --name Yes Badge name --badge-type-id Yes Badge type: 1 (gold), 2 (silver), 3 (bronze)

docs.title: update
docs.text:
Update a badge.

discourse badges update 10 --name "Top Contributor" --badge-type-id 2
Argument Description BADGE_ID Badge ID (integer) Option Required Description --name Yes Badge name --badge-type-id Yes Badge type ID

docs.title: delete
docs.text:
Delete a badge. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes is passed.

discourse badges delete 10 --yes
Argument Description BADGE_ID Badge ID (integer) Option Description --yes Skip confirmation prompt

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List all badges
discourse badges list

# Check a user's badges
discourse badges user alice -o json | jq '.[].name'

# Create a bronze badge
discourse badges create --name "First Post" --badge-type-id 3

# Upgrade badge to silver
discourse badges update 10 --name "First Post" --badge-type-id 2

docs.title: categories
docs.text:
Manage categories.

discourse categories <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all categories.

discourse categories list
discourse categories list --include-subcategories
Option Description --include-subcategories Include subcategories in the listing

docs.title: get
docs.text:
Get a category by ID.

discourse categories get 5
Argument Description CATEGORY_ID Category ID (integer)

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create a new category.

discourse categories create --name "Announcements" --color "0088CC" --text-color "FFFFFF"
Option Required Description --name Yes Category name --color No Category color (hex, without #) --text-color No Text color (hex, without #) --slug No Category slug --parent-category-id No Parent category ID (for subcategories)

docs.title: update
docs.text:
Update a category.

discourse categories update 5 --name "News & Announcements" --color "FF0000"
Argument Description CATEGORY_ID Category ID (integer) Option Required Description --name Yes Category name --color No Category color (hex) --text-color No Text color (hex) --slug No Category slug --parent-category-id No Parent category ID

docs.title: topics
docs.text:
List topics in a specific category.

discourse categories topics general 5
Argument Description SLUG Category slug CATEGORY_ID Category ID (integer)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List all categories with subcategories
discourse categories list --include-subcategories

# Create a subcategory
discourse categories create --name "Bug Reports" --parent-category-id 5 --color "CC0000"

# Get category details as JSON
discourse categories get 5 -o json

# Browse topics in a category
discourse categories topics announcements 3

docs.title: config
docs.text:
Manage Discourse CLI configuration and profiles.

discourse config <subcommand>

docs.title: init
docs.text:
Create configuration file interactively.

discourse config init
discourse config init --profile staging
Option Description --profile Profile name to create (e.g. staging). Creates a named profile.

Prompts for URL, API key, and API username. If a config file already exists and no profile is specified, asks for confirmation before overwriting.

docs.title: show
docs.text:
Show current configuration with masked API key.

discourse config show
discourse config show --profile production
Option Description --profile Profile name to show

docs.title: validate
docs.text:
Validate configuration by testing the connection to the Discourse instance.

discourse config validate
discourse config validate --profile staging
Option Description --profile Profile name to validate

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all configured profiles.

discourse config list

Shows all profile names with the default profile marked.

docs.title: use
docs.text:
Set the default profile.

discourse config use production
Argument Description NAME Profile name to set as default

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Initial setup
discourse config init

# Add a staging profile
discourse config init --profile staging

# Verify connection
discourse config validate

# Switch between profiles
discourse config use staging
discourse config list

docs.title: groups
docs.text:
Manage groups.

discourse groups <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all groups.

discourse groups list

docs.title: get
docs.text:
Get a group by name.

discourse groups get moderators
Argument Description NAME Group name

docs.title: get-by-id
docs.text:
Get a group by numeric ID.

discourse groups get-by-id 42
Argument Description GROUP_ID Group ID

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create a new group.

discourse groups create --name "beta-testers" --full-name "Beta Testers" --visibility-level 2
Option Required Description --name Yes Group name --full-name No Full display name --bio-raw No Group bio (Markdown) --visibility-level No Visibility level (integer) --primary-group No Set as primary group for members

docs.title: update
docs.text:
Update a group.

discourse groups update 42 --full-name "Senior Beta Testers"
Argument Description GROUP_ID Group ID (integer) Option Description --name Group name --full-name Full display name --bio-raw Group bio --visibility-level Visibility level

docs.title: delete
docs.text:
Delete a group. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes is passed.

discourse groups delete 42 --yes
Argument Description GROUP_ID Group ID (integer) Option Description --yes Skip confirmation prompt

docs.title: members
docs.text:
List members of a group.

discourse groups members moderators
Argument Description NAME Group name

docs.title: add-members
docs.text:
Add members to a group.

discourse groups add-members 42 --usernames "alice,bob,charlie"
Argument Description GROUP_ID Group ID (integer) Option Required Description --usernames Yes Comma-separated usernames to add

docs.title: remove-members
docs.text:
Remove members from a group.

discourse groups remove-members 42 --usernames "charlie"
Argument Description GROUP_ID Group ID (integer) Option Required Description --usernames Yes Comma-separated usernames to remove

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Create a group and add members
discourse groups create --name "project-alpha" --full-name "Project Alpha Team"
discourse groups add-members 42 --usernames "alice,bob"

# List members as JSON
discourse groups members moderators -o json | jq '.[].username'

# Remove a member
discourse groups remove-members 42 --usernames "bob"

docs.title: invites
docs.text:
Manage invitations.

discourse invites <subcommand>

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create an invite link or send an invite email.

discourse invites create --email alice@example.com
discourse invites create --max-redemptions 10 --group-names "beta-testers" --expires-at "2026-03-01"
Option Description --email Email address to invite --skip-email Create link only, don't send email --custom-message Custom invite message --max-redemptions Maximum number of redemptions allowed --topic-id Topic to invite to --group-names Comma-separated group names to add invitee to --expires-at Expiry date (ISO format)

docs.title: bulk
docs.text:
Create multiple invites at once.

discourse invites bulk --email "emails.txt" --group-names "newcomers"
Option Description --email Email list (one per line in file) --group-names Comma-separated group names --custom-message Custom message

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Invite a user to the forum
discourse invites create --email new-user@example.com --custom-message "Welcome!"

# Create a reusable invite link
discourse invites create --skip-email --max-redemptions 50 --expires-at "2026-06-01"

# Invite users to a specific group
discourse invites create --email alice@example.com --group-names "beta-testers,moderators"

docs.title: messages
docs.text:
Manage private messages.

discourse messages <subcommand>

docs.title: inbox
docs.text:
List a user's private message inbox.

discourse messages inbox alice
Argument Description USERNAME Username

docs.title: sent
docs.text:
List a user's sent private messages.

discourse messages sent alice
Argument Description USERNAME Username

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Send a private message.

discourse messages create --title "Hello" --raw "How are you?" --target-recipients "bob,charlie"
Option Required Description --title Yes Message subject --raw Yes Message content (Markdown) --target-recipients Yes Comma-separated usernames

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Check inbox
discourse messages inbox system

# Send a message to multiple users
discourse messages create --title "Meeting" --raw "Let's meet tomorrow." --target-recipients "alice,bob"

# List sent messages as JSON
discourse messages sent admin -o json

docs.title: notifications
docs.text:
Manage notifications.

discourse notifications <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List notifications for the current API user.

discourse notifications list

docs.title: mark-read
docs.text:
Mark notifications as read. Without --id, marks all notifications as read.

# Mark all as read
discourse notifications mark-read

# Mark a specific notification
discourse notifications mark-read --id 456
Option Description --id Specific notification ID. Omit to mark all as read.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List unread notifications
discourse notifications list -o json | jq '[.[] | select(.read == false)]'

# Mark all notifications as read
discourse notifications mark-read

docs.title: posts
docs.text:
Manage posts.

discourse posts <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List latest posts across all topics.

discourse posts list
discourse posts list --before 500
Option Description --before List posts before this post ID (for pagination)

docs.title: get
docs.text:
Get a post by ID.

discourse posts get 42
Argument Description POST_ID Post ID

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create a new post (reply to a topic).

discourse posts create --topic-id 123 --raw "Great discussion!"
discourse posts create --topic-id 123 --raw "I agree with you." --reply-to 2
Option Required Description --topic-id No Topic ID to reply to --raw Yes Post content (Markdown) --reply-to No Post number to reply to within the topic

docs.title: update
docs.text:
Update a post's content.

discourse posts update 42 --raw "Updated content here." --edit-reason "Fixed typo"
Argument Description POST_ID Post ID Option Description --raw New post content --edit-reason Reason for the edit

docs.title: delete
docs.text:
Delete a post. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes is passed.

discourse posts delete 42 --yes
discourse posts delete 42 --force --yes
Argument Description POST_ID Post ID (integer) Option Description --force Force destroy (permanent deletion) --yes Skip confirmation prompt

docs.title: lock
docs.text:
Lock or unlock a post to prevent editing.

discourse posts lock 42 --locked
discourse posts lock 42 --unlocked
Argument Description POST_ID Post ID Option Required Description --locked / --unlocked Yes Lock or unlock the post

docs.title: replies
docs.text:
Get replies to a specific post.

discourse posts replies 42
Argument Description POST_ID Post ID

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Reply to a topic
discourse posts create --topic-id 123 --raw "Thanks for sharing!"

# Edit a post with a reason
discourse posts update 42 --raw "Corrected version." --edit-reason "Fixed formatting"

# Get replies as JSON
discourse posts replies 42 -o json

# Lock a staff post
discourse posts lock 42 --locked

docs.title: search
docs.text:
Search topics, posts, and users.

discourse search <QUERY>

This is a top-level command, not a command group.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description QUERY Search query string

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --page Page number for paginated results

docs.title: Output
docs.text: By default, search results display matching topics. If no topics match but posts do, posts are shown instead. Use -o json for the full response including all result types.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Basic search
discourse search "welcome guide"

# Paginate results
discourse search "installation" --page 2

# Get full search results as JSON
discourse search "docker" -o json | jq '.topics[].title'

# Use Discourse search operators
discourse search "status:open category:support"
discourse search "@alice order:latest"

docs.title: site
docs.text:
Site information and settings.

discourse site <subcommand>

docs.title: info
docs.text:
Get basic site information (title, description, version, etc.).

discourse site info

docs.title: full
docs.text:
Get the full site configuration including all settings, categories, trust levels, and more.

discourse site full

Tip

The full command returns a large amount of data. Use -o json with jq to extract specific fields.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Show site name and version
discourse site info

# Get site version from JSON
discourse site info -o json | jq '.version'

# Export full site configuration
discourse site full -o json > site-config.json

# List all trust levels
discourse site full -o json | jq '.trust_levels'

docs.title: tags
docs.text:
Manage tags and tag groups.

discourse tags <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all tags.

discourse tags list

docs.title: get
docs.text:
Get a tag by name, including associated topics.

discourse tags get python
Argument Description NAME Tag name

docs.title: groups
docs.text:
List all tag groups.

discourse tags groups

docs.title: group-get
docs.text:
Get a tag group by ID.

discourse tags group-get 3
Argument Description GROUP_ID Tag group ID

docs.title: group-create
docs.text:
Create a new tag group.

discourse tags group-create --name "Programming Languages"
Option Required Description --name Yes Tag group name

docs.title: group-update
docs.text:
Update a tag group.

discourse tags group-update 3 --name "Languages"
Argument Description GROUP_ID Tag group ID Option Description --name New tag group name

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List all tags as JSON
discourse tags list -o json | jq '.[].name'

# View topics tagged with "python"
discourse tags get python -o json | jq '.topic_list.topics[].title'

# Manage tag groups
discourse tags groups
discourse tags group-create --name "Frameworks"
discourse tags group-update 5 --name "Web Frameworks"

docs.title: topics
docs.text:
Manage topics.

discourse topics <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List latest topics.

discourse topics list
discourse topics list --per-page 50 --order activity --ascending
Option Description --order Sort order field --ascending Sort ascending --per-page Results per page

docs.title: top
docs.text:
List top topics by time period.

discourse topics top --period monthly
Option Description --period Time period: all, yearly, quarterly, monthly, weekly, daily --per-page Results per page

docs.title: get
docs.text:
Get a topic by ID.

discourse topics get 123
Argument Description TOPIC_ID Topic ID

docs.title: get-by-external-id
docs.text:
Get a topic by external ID.

discourse topics get-by-external-id ext-456
Argument Description EXTERNAL_ID External ID

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create a new topic.

discourse topics create --title "Welcome" --raw "Hello everyone!" --category 5
Option Required Description --title Yes Topic title --raw Yes Post content (Markdown) --category No Category ID --external-id No External ID

docs.title: update
docs.text:
Update a topic's title or category.

discourse topics update 123 --title "Updated Title" --category-id 7
Argument Description TOPIC_ID Topic ID Option Description --title New title --category-id New category ID

docs.title: delete
docs.text:
Delete a topic. Prompts for confirmation unless --yes is passed.

discourse topics delete 123 --yes
Argument Description TOPIC_ID Topic ID Option Description --yes Skip confirmation prompt

docs.title: status
docs.text:
Update topic status (close, archive, pin, visibility).

discourse topics status 123 --status closed --enabled
discourse topics status 123 --status pinned --enabled --until 2026-03-01
Argument Description TOPIC_ID Topic ID Option Required Description --status Yes Status to change: closed, archived, visible, pinned --enabled / --disabled Yes Enable or disable the status --until No Until date (for pinned topics)

docs.title: timer
docs.text:
Set a topic timer (auto-close, auto-open, etc.).

discourse topics timer 123 --status-type close --time "2026-03-01 12:00"
Argument Description TOPIC_ID Topic ID Option Description --time Timer time --status-type Timer type (e.g. close, open) --category-id Move to category ID (for category-move timers)

docs.title: invite
docs.text:
Invite a user to a topic by username or email.

discourse topics invite 123 --user alice
discourse topics invite 123 --email bob@example.com
Argument Description TOPIC_ID Topic ID Option Description --user Username to invite --email Email to invite

docs.title: bookmark
docs.text:
Bookmark a topic.

discourse topics bookmark 123
Argument Description TOPIC_ID Topic ID

docs.title: notification-level
docs.text:
Set the notification level for a topic.

discourse topics notification-level 123 --level 3
Argument Description TOPIC_ID Topic ID Option Required Description --level Yes Notification level: 0 (muted), 1 (normal), 2 (tracking), 3 (watching)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List top topics of the week
discourse topics top --period weekly

# Create a pinned announcement
discourse topics create --title "Maintenance Window" --raw "Scheduled for Saturday." --category 1
discourse topics status 456 --status pinned --enabled --until 2026-03-01

# Auto-close a topic after a date
discourse topics timer 123 --status-type close --time "2026-04-01"

# Get topic as JSON for scripting
discourse topics get 123 -o json | jq '.title'

docs.title: uploads
docs.text:
Manage file uploads.

discourse uploads <subcommand>

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create an upload.

discourse uploads create --type composer
discourse uploads create --type avatar --user-id 42 --synchronous
Option Required Description --type Yes Upload type: avatar, profile_background, card_background, custom_emoji, composer --user-id No User ID for the upload --synchronous No Perform a synchronous upload

docs.title: create-multipart
docs.text:
Initiate a multipart upload for large files.

discourse uploads create-multipart --upload-type composer --file-name "large-file.zip" --file-size 104857600
Option Required Description --upload-type Yes Upload type --file-name Yes File name --file-size Yes File size in bytes

docs.title: complete-external
docs.text:
Complete an external upload after all parts have been uploaded.

discourse uploads complete-external --unique-identifier "abc-123-def"
Option Required Description --unique-identifier Yes Upload identifier from the create-multipart response

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Simple upload
discourse uploads create --type composer

# Multipart upload workflow
discourse uploads create-multipart --upload-type composer --file-name "backup.zip" --file-size 52428800
# ... upload parts ...
discourse uploads complete-external --unique-identifier "abc-123"

docs.title: users
docs.text:
Manage users.

discourse users <subcommand>

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List users (admin endpoint).

discourse users list
discourse users list --flag staff --order username --asc
Option Description --flag Filter by flag: active, new, staff, suspended, blocked, suspect --order Sort order field --asc Sort ascending --page Page number --show-emails Show email addresses --email Filter by email --ip Filter by IP address

docs.title: get
docs.text:
Get user details by username.

discourse users get alice
Argument Description USERNAME Username to look up

docs.title: get-by-id
docs.text:
Get user details by numeric ID (admin endpoint).

discourse users get-by-id 42
Argument Description USER_ID User ID (integer)

docs.title: create
docs.text:
Create a new user. Prompts for password if not provided.

discourse users create --name "Alice" --email alice@example.com --username alice --password secret
Option Required Description --name Yes Display name --email Yes Email address --username Yes Username --password Yes Password (prompted if omitted) --active / --no-active No Activate immediately --approved / --no-approved No Approve immediately

docs.title: update
docs.text:
Update a user.

discourse users update alice --name "Alice Smith"
Argument Description USERNAME Username to update Option Description --name New display name

docs.title: delete
docs.text:
Delete a user (admin endpoint). Prompts for confirmation unless --yes is passed.

discourse users delete 42 --yes
discourse users delete 42 --delete-posts --block-email
Argument Description USER_ID User ID (integer) Option Description --delete-posts Also delete the user's posts --block-email Block the user's email --block-urls Block the user's URLs --block-ip Block the user's IP --yes Skip confirmation prompt

docs.title: emails
docs.text:
Get a user's email addresses (admin endpoint).

discourse users emails alice
Argument Description USERNAME Username

docs.title: update-username
docs.text:
Change a user's username.

discourse users update-username alice --new-username alice_smith
Argument Description USERNAME Current username Option Required Description --new-username Yes New username

docs.title: update-email
docs.text:
Change a user's email.

discourse users update-email alice --email new@example.com
Argument Description USERNAME Username Option Required Description --email Yes New email address

docs.title: reset-password
docs.text:
Send a password reset email.

discourse users reset-password alice
Argument Description LOGIN Username or email

docs.title: actions
docs.text:
List user actions.

discourse users actions alice --filter 1
Argument Description USERNAME Username Option Required Description --filter Yes Action type filter (e.g. 1 for likes) --offset No Offset (default: 0)

docs.title: by-external-id
docs.text:
Get user by external ID.

discourse users by-external-id ext-12345
Argument Description EXTERNAL_ID External ID

docs.title: public
docs.text:
List the public user directory.

discourse users public --period monthly --order likes_received
Option Required Description --period Yes Time period: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly, all --order Yes Sort by: likes_received, likes_given, topic_count, post_count, topics_entered, posts_read, days_visited --asc No Sort ascending --page No Page number

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List active users
discourse users list --flag active

# Get user details as JSON
discourse users get alice -o json

# Create and activate a user
discourse users create --name "Bob" --email bob@example.com --username bob --active

# Delete user and block their email
discourse users delete 42 --delete-posts --block-email --yes

# Find user by external SSO ID
discourse users by-external-id sso-abc123
